---
title: "Figure 4 (bias) — the first moment across the design space: LSE, NR, R, IR"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
THE FIRST-MOMENT HALF of the old Figure 4, split in two (Luciano, 2026-07-23) because four algorithms
carrying both moments would run to sixteen map rows. This half: the ellipses plus the
DISTORTION-INDUCED BIAS, from battery_sim_G anchored at theta_sim. The second-moment half is
figure_4_distortion.Rmd, which carries the same ellipse block.

The bias CANNOT come from the pool files: evaluated at the optimum it is zero by construction,
because the score vanishes there.

BLOCK A, the ellipses, is the referent: a bias map is a colour field over a log10 displacement, and
nobody has an intuition for 0.13. The cloud says it in the form a reader already knows — here is
where your estimates land, here is where the truth is, here is where the distortion theory PREDICTS
they land (open circle = truth + predicted bias, a prediction and not a re-description of the cloud).

PLANE: one panel per N_ch (columns), x = Delta*k_off, y = raw instrumental noise. ROWS are parameter
(outer) then algorithm (inner), so the four rungs of a parameter are adjacent and the ladder reads
top-to-bottom.

WHICH DIRECTION: the primary render is N[ch], the direction that carries the bias (R's N_ch bias runs
0.126/0.148/0.138/0.138 log10 across the four decades against IR's ~0, while k_off's bias is ~0 for
both). The both-directions variant is also written, because the two parameters carry OPPOSITE signs
and only showing both makes verifiable that a scalar averaged over parameters cancels them.

All machinery, traps and scales live in figure_4_common.R, shared with the distortion half.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r common, include=FALSE}
source("figure_4_common.R")
```

```{r ellipses, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 9.5}
gA <- build_gA()
gA
```

```{r assemble}
# one assembler, the parameter roster is the argument. Block A is shared and unchanged: an ellipse
# needs two axes, so it stays on the (k_off, N_ch) pair whatever the maps show.
assemble_bias <- function(params) {
  hA <- 2.30 * max(1, length(ELL_ALGOS))
  hB <- map_height(length(params), 0.45)          # 0.90 in per 2-decade row
  g <- btitle("A   grouped MLE distributions") /
       gA /
       btitle("B   distortion-induced bias") /
       mapblock(bias, function(d) d$Bconf, BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LIN, params, top = TRUE, bottom = TRUE,
                iso_sol = BIAS_SOL, iso_dsh = BIAS_DSH) /
       colorbar(BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LAB, "bias (log10 units)", BIAS_LIN,
                sol = BIAS_SOL, dsh = BIAS_DSH)
  hs <- c(0.16, hA, 0.16, hB, 0.28)
  list(g = g + plot_layout(heights = hs), h = sum(hs))
}
```

```{r render-primary, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 12}
# PRIMARY: N[ch] alone, the direction that carries the bias. Four map rows, one per algorithm.
prim <- assemble_bias("Num_ch_mean")
prim$g
ggsave("Figure_4_bias.pdf", prim$g, width = 7.0, height = prim$h)
cat("Figure_4_bias.pdf: bias on N_ch; height", round(prim$h, 2), "in\n")
```

```{r render-both-params, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 18}
# VARIANT: both directions. k_off's bias is ~0 for every algorithm, which is exactly the point —
# the moments live in DIFFERENT parameters, so a scalar averaged over parameters reports a faithful
# algorithm. Only this render makes that verifiable.
both <- assemble_bias(PARAM_LONG)
both$g
ggsave("Figure_4_bias_both_params.pdf", both$g, width = 7.0, height = both$h)
cat("Figure_4_bias_both_params.pdf: k_off and N_ch; height", round(both$h, 2), "in\n")
```

```{r caption-numbers}
cat("\n=== Figure 4 (bias) caption numbers ===\n")
cat("\nBIAS (log10 units), median over the 7 intervals, noise 0.1\n")
print(as.data.frame(bias %>% filter(noise == "0.1") %>%
        group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>% summarise(med = median(Bconf), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = med)), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nEXTENT: fraction of (interval, noise) cells whose interval excludes 0\n")
print(as.data.frame(bias %>% group_by(param, algo, Num_ch) %>%
        summarise(f = mean(Bconf != 0), .groups = "drop") %>%
        mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = f)), digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)
```
